expectations.text: Response creates Elysia routes with /api/recipes prefix
expectations.evidence: Creates routes with .get('/api/recipes') and .post('/api/recipes') directly on the app instance. Uses inline path strings rather than the prefix pattern, but routes resolve to /api/recipes.

expectations.text: Response uses t.Object for Typebox query/body validation
expectations.evidence: GET route uses t.Object for query params with q, cuisine, page, limit. POST route uses t.Object for body with title, description, ingredients, cook_time_minutes, cuisine.

expectations.text: Response validates cuisine as enum/union type
expectations.evidence: Both query and body schemas define cuisine as t.Union([t.Literal('italian'), t.Literal('mexican'), t.Literal('japanese'), t.Literal('indian'), t.Literal('american')]). Duplicated inline rather than extracted to a reusable schema.

expectations.text: Response includes pagination params with defaults
expectations.evidence: Query schema defines page and limit as t.Optional(t.String()) without defaults. Pagination defaults are handled manually in the handler with 'Number(query.page) || 1' and 'Number(query.limit) || 20'. Does not use t.Numeric with default parameter for proper Typebox-level defaults.

expectations.text: Response defines error response schemas for 400/404
expectations.evidence: POST route defines response: { 400: t.Object({ error: t.String() }), 404: t.Object({ error: t.String() }) }. Global onError handler handles VALIDATION (400), NOT_FOUND (404), and default (500).
